description: Address the open issues tracked in `.agent/notes/sqlite-review-issues.md` from the multi-agent adversarial review of the rivetkit-sqlite VFS, depot conveyer/cold-tier/PITR, and driver test suite. For each story: (1) confirm or add a failing regression test that pins the buggy behavior, (2) implement a CLEAN root-cause fix per the issue's `Fix sketch`, (3) confirm the test passes. Read `.agent/notes/sqlite-review-issues.md` for the full per-issue context (Failure mode, Fix sketch, Verified evidence, Regression test). Bug fixes must be CLEAN, not tactical patches: see the `Clean Fixes — Do Not Apply Hacky Patches` section in this directory's CLAUDE.md. If a fix sketch turns out to be wrong on closer inspection, update the issue file and the story `notes` before diverging. Verification: `cargo check -p <pkg>` for Rust; `cargo test -p <pkg> <test_name>` for individual tests; TS work runs `pnpm run check` and the relevant Vitest filter plus `pnpm run check:wait-for-comments`. Do NOT run `cargo fmt` or `./scripts/cargo/fix.sh`. Use conventional-commit messages with no co-author line.

userStories.title: S1 — Replace set_last_error with clear_last_error in commit_atomic_write success path
userStories.description: As a depot client polling `take_last_kv_error()` after a commit, I should not see a 'success' string framed as an error.
acceptanceCriteria: Existing test `commit_atomic_write_clears_last_error_on_success` in `rivetkit-rust/packages/rivetkit-sqlite/tests/inline/vfs.rs` fails before the fix and passes after
`vfs.rs:1144-1148` no longer calls `set_last_error("post-commit atomic write succeeded: ...")`; replace with `clear_last_error()` to match the file's success-path pattern (lines 1649, 1797)
If size diagnostics are still useful, emit them via `tracing::debug!` with structured fields; do not encode them in `last_error`
`cargo check -p rivetkit-sqlite` passes
`cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite commit_atomic_write_clears_last_error_on_success` passes
userStories.notes: Pre-fix regression confirmed with `cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite commit_atomic_write_clears_last_error_on_success`: failed because `take_last_kv_error()` returned `Some("post-commit atomic write succeeded: requested_db_size_pages=1, returned_db_size_pages=1")`. After replacing the success-path `set_last_error(...)` with `clear_last_error()` and moving requested size into structured debug fields, `cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite commit_atomic_write_clears_last_error_on_success` and `cargo check -p rivetkit-sqlite` pass.

userStories.title: S2 — vfs_delete on main DB resets in-memory state or returns explicit error
userStories.description: As SQLite calling xDelete on a main DB path, I should observe either a loud error or a fully reset VFS state — not stale pages claiming success.
acceptanceCriteria: Existing test `vfs_delete_main_db_resets_in_memory_state` in `tests/inline/vfs.rs` fails before the fix and passes after

Choose ONE contract for `vfs_delete` (`vfs.rs:1986-2011`) when `path == ctx.actor_id`: either return `SQLITE_IOERR_DELETE` (loud failure) OR reset `db_size_pages = 0`, `page_cache.clear()`, `write_buffer.dirty.clear()` under a write lock
Document the chosen contract in a one-line comment on `vfs_delete`
`cargo check -p rivetkit-sqlite` passes
`cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite vfs_delete_main_db_resets_in_memory_state` passes
userStories.notes: Pre-fix regression confirmed with `cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite vfs_delete_main_db_resets_in_memory_state`: failed because main-db xDelete returned `SQLITE_OK` while `db_size_pages=2`. Chose the loud-failure contract because xDelete cannot remove persisted depot state; `vfs_delete` now sets `last_error` to `main database deletion is unsupported` and returns `SQLITE_IOERR_DELETE` for `path == ctx.actor_id`. After the fix, `cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite vfs_delete_main_db_resets_in_memory_state` and `cargo check -p rivetkit-sqlite` pass.

userStories.title: S35 — Rename SQLITE_RESTORE_POINT_COUNT_PER_NAMESPACE constant to _PER_BUCKET
userStories.description: As a depot maintainer, I want metric identifiers to match their Prometheus names after the US-080 namespace→bucket rename.
acceptanceCriteria: Rename the Rust ident `SQLITE_RESTORE_POINT_COUNT_PER_NAMESPACE` → `SQLITE_RESTORE_POINT_COUNT_PER_BUCKET` at `engine/packages/depot/src/conveyer/metrics.rs:170`
Update all call sites; the Prometheus metric name (`sqlite_restore_point_count_per_bucket`) does not change
`cargo check -p depot` passes
userStories.notes: Renamed Rust ident `SQLITE_RESTORE_POINT_COUNT_PER_NAMESPACE` to `SQLITE_RESTORE_POINT_COUNT_PER_BUCKET` in `engine/packages/depot/src/conveyer/metrics.rs` while leaving the Prometheus metric name `sqlite_restore_point_count_per_bucket` unchanged. Verified with `rg -n "SQLITE_RESTORE_POINT_COUNT_PER_NAMESPACE|SQLITE_RESTORE_POINT_COUNT_PER_BUCKET" /home/nathan/r2/engine/packages/depot/src /home/nathan/r2/scripts/ralph/prd.json` and `cargo check -p depot`.

userStories.title: S33 — Remove dead per-commit read of branch_manifest_cold_drained_txid_key
userStories.description: As a depot maintainer, I want to stop paying an FDB get per commit for a key no production code writes.
acceptanceCriteria: Confirm via grep that no production code writes `branch_manifest_cold_drained_txid_key` (only `tests/burst_mode.rs:41` writes it); record the grep output in story notes
Delete the read in `engine/packages/depot/src/burst_mode.rs:54-65` (was line 57) and `engine/packages/depot/src/conveyer/commit/dirty.rs:126-137` (was line 129)
Existing depot tests pass: `cargo test -p depot`
`cargo check -p depot` passes
userStories.notes: Confirmed with `rg -n "branch_manifest_cold_drained_txid_key|set.*cold_drained|atomic.*cold_drained" /home/nathan/r2/engine/packages/depot --glob '!target/**'`: no production code writes the legacy key; remaining references are the key helper, key-layout test, and `tests/burst_mode.rs` writes that prove the legacy key is ignored. Removed the production legacy-key reads from `src/burst_mode.rs` and `src/conveyer/commit/dirty.rs`; burst mode now derives cold lag from workflow `CMP/root.cold_watermark_txid`, and commit dirty cleanup uses the required workflow compaction root. Verified `cargo check -p depot` and `cargo test -p depot` pass.

Update its tests to exercise the live reclaimer path at `reclaimer.rs:195-199` (uses `compare_and_clear`) instead
`cargo test -p depot` passes
`cargo check -p depot` passes
userStories.notes: Confirmed with `rg -n "delete_expired_pitr_interval_coverage" /home/nathan/r2/engine/packages/depot/src /home/nathan/r2/engine/packages/depot/tests --glob '!target/**'`: before the fix, only the helper itself and `tests/conveyer_pitr_interval.rs` referenced it; after deletion the same search returns no matches. Removed the dead helper from `src/conveyer/pitr_interval.rs` and removed its direct helper test/import from `tests/conveyer_pitr_interval.rs`; live expiry behavior remains covered by reclaimer tests that exercise `expired_pitr_interval_rows` with `udb::compare_and_clear` in `src/workflows/compaction/reclaimer.rs`. userStories.title: S36 — Remove dead takeover scan of legacy database-scoped keys
userStories.description: As a depot maintainer, I want to remove the no-op debug-only takeover scan while keeping the legacy keyfns that pegboard v1 actors still depend on.
acceptanceCriteria: Confirm only `pegboard/src/actor_sqlite.rs:33,44-46,170` and tests reference legacy keyfns (`meta_head_key`, `delta_prefix`, `pidx_delta_prefix`, `shard_prefix`); record evidence in notes
Remove the legacy-key portion of the takeover scan at `engine/packages/depot/src/takeover.rs:73,80-82` so it no longer scans keys that production never writes
Keep `meta_head_key`/`delta_prefix`/`pidx_delta_prefix`/`shard_prefix` in `engine/packages/depot/src/conveyer/keys.rs` (load-bearing for v1 pegboard actors); add a comment noting they are v1-only
`cargo test -p depot` passes
`cargo check -p depot -p pegboard` passes
userStories.notes: Confirmed with `rg -n "depot_keys::(meta_head_key|delta_prefix|pidx_delta_prefix|shard_prefix)|\\b(meta_head_key|delta_prefix|pidx_delta_prefix|shard_prefix)\\(" engine/packages --glob '!target/**'`: production references after the fix are only `engine/packages/pegboard/src/actor_sqlite.rs:33,44-46,170`; remaining matches are definitions/internal helper use and tests. Removed the legacy database-scoped key reads/scans from `engine/packages/depot/src/takeover.rs`; `reconcile_inner` is now an explicit no-op until a branch-scoped invariant check exists. Kept the legacy key helpers and documented them as v1 pegboard compatibility. Updated `tests/takeover.rs` to assert legacy database-scoped rows are ignored. The worktree also contained pre-existing depot tier-matrix test changes; the full depot suite passed with them included. Verified `cargo test -p depot --test takeover legacy_database_scoped_rows_are_ignored`, `cargo test -p depot`, and `cargo check -p depot -p pegboard`.

userStories.title: S5 — Document xSync durability contract on io_sync
userStories.description: As a future maintainer reading io_sync, I should know that durability is delegated to depot's sqlite_commit ack and what would break if that ack ever pre-returns.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a doc comment above `io_sync` (`vfs.rs:1818-1833`) stating: xSync returns once `ctx.flush_dirty_pages()` resolves; durability of those bytes is delegated to depot's `sqlite_commit` reply; if pegboard-envoy ever pre-acks before FDB tx commit, xSync's durability contract is broken
`cargo check -p rivetkit-sqlite` passes
userStories.notes: Added a doc comment above `io_sync` documenting that xSync returns after `ctx.flush_dirty_pages()` resolves, that byte durability is delegated to depot's `sqlite_commit` reply, and that pre-acking before the FDB tx commit would break xSync durability. Verified `cargo check -p rivetkit-sqlite` passes with existing Rust 2024 unsafe-operation warnings.

userStories.title: S8 — Add regression test for embedded NUL in SQL string
userStories.description: As a depot maintainer, I want to pin the contract that `query_statement` rejects SQL with embedded NUL bytes via the CString error chain.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a test in `rivetkit-rust/packages/rivetkit-sqlite/tests/query_text_nul.rs` (or `tests/inline/query.rs`) that calls `query_statement(db, "SELECT 1\0; --", None)` and asserts the returned `anyhow::Error` chain mentions the embedded NUL
Test pins the rejection at `src/query.rs:48` (`CString::new(sql)`)
`cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite` passes
userStories.notes: Added `query_sql_with_embedded_nul_is_rejected` in `rivetkit-rust/packages/rivetkit-sqlite/tests/query_text_nul.rs`. The test calls `query_statement(db, "SELECT 1\0; --", None)` and asserts the returned anyhow error chain contains the `CString::new(sql)` NUL-byte rejection, while the existing bound TEXT NUL round-trip test still passes. Verified `cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite query_sql_with_embedded_nul_is_rejected`, `cargo check -p rivetkit-sqlite`, and `cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite`.

userStories.title: S7 — Re-verify whether seed_main_page is dead, then close the issue cleanly
userStories.description: As a depot maintainer, I need to resolve the contradiction between the verifier's claim that `seed_main_page` refreshes `page_size` from the header and the cargo-check warning that the helper is dead.
acceptanceCriteria: Determine via grep + `cargo check -p rivetkit-sqlite` whether `seed_main_page` (`vfs.rs:744-747`) and `fetch_initial_main_page` (`vfs.rs:1237`) are actually called; record the answer in story notes

If dead: wire them in (call from `VfsContext::new`) so `page_size` is refreshed from the header on first non-empty fetch, and add a test that opens with a non-default page_size and asserts `xFileSize` returns the correct byte count
If live: drop S7 from the issue tracker — set its Status to `wontfix` in `.agent/notes/sqlite-review-issues.md` with verified-live evidence
`cargo check -p rivetkit-sqlite` passes
userStories.notes: Verified live with `rg -n "seed_main_page|fetch_initial_main_page" /home/nathan/r2/rivetkit-rust/packages/rivetkit-sqlite /home/nathan/r2/.agent/notes/sqlite-review-issues.md /home/nathan/r2/scripts/ralph --glob '!target/**'`: `VfsContext::new` calls `fetch_initial_main_page`, and its page flows into `VfsState::seed_main_page`. `cargo check -p rivetkit-sqlite` passed and did not report either helper as dead, only the existing Rust 2024 unsafe-operation warnings. Set S7 to `wontfix` in `.agent/notes/sqlite-review-issues.md` because the production open path already refreshes `page_size` from the fetched DB header.

userStories.title: S3 — Drop write lock around moka cache insert in resolve_pages
userStories.description: As a concurrent reader on the VFS state, I should not be blocked by a write guard that only does `moka::Cache::insert` (internally synced).
acceptanceCriteria: At `vfs.rs:999-1009`, drop the `let state = self.state.write();` guard or downgrade to `state.read()`; rely on `moka::Cache`'s internal synchronization
Clean up the indentation in the loop (verifier flagged it as off-by-one consistent with a refactor leftover)
Existing tests `cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite` pass — concurrency is not regressed
`cargo check -p rivetkit-sqlite` passes
userStories.notes: Removed the post-fetch `state.write()` guard in `resolve_pages` by cloning the internally synchronized `moka::Cache` handle under a short read and inserting fetched pages through that cache handle. Cleaned up the shifted `match`/loop indentation around the cache-fill path. Verified `cargo check -p rivetkit-sqlite` and `cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite` pass with existing Rust 2024 unsafe-operation warnings.

userStories.title: S19 — Move test fixtures out of vfs.rs into tests/inline/vfs_support.rs
userStories.description: As a maintainer reading vfs.rs, I should see only production code; test fixtures belong under `tests/inline/`.
acceptanceCriteria: Identify the inline `#[cfg(test)]` fixture blocks in `rivetkit-rust/packages/rivetkit-sqlite/src/vfs.rs` (DirectStorage, DirectActorPages, DirectTransportHooks, MockProtocol, helpers `compaction_signals`, `take_commit_error`)
Move them to `rivetkit-rust/packages/rivetkit-sqlite/tests/inline/vfs_support.rs` (new file) referenced from the existing `mod tests;` shim
Keep only the path-shimmed `#[cfg(test)] #[path = "..."] mod tests;` line in `src/vfs.rs`
`cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite` passes
`cargo check -p rivetkit-sqlite` passes
userStories.notes: Moved the test-only VFS direct-storage and mock-protocol fixtures (`DirectStorage`, `DirectActorPages`, `DirectTransportHooks`, `MockProtocol`, and helper conversion/error functions) out of `src/vfs.rs` into `tests/inline/vfs_support.rs`. The existing path-shimmed `mod tests;` in `src/vfs.rs` now references the support module through `tests::...`, keeping the fixture definitions under `tests/inline/` while preserving private-module test access. Verified `cargo check -p rivetkit-sqlite` and `cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite` pass with existing Rust 2024 unsafe-operation warnings.

userStories.title: S10 — Restructure depot/workflows/ to one-module-per-workflow
userStories.description: As a depot maintainer, I want each workflow to live in its own file (or its own module) named after the workflow, with shared/util code outside `workflows/`.
acceptanceCriteria: Identify the actual workflow names of `cold.rs`, `companion.rs`, `hot.rs`, `manager.rs`, `reclaimer.rs` in `engine/packages/depot/src/workflows/compaction/`; record the names in story notes
Promote each workflow file to `engine/packages/depot/src/workflows/{wf_name}.rs` (single-file) or `workflows/{wf_name}/mod.rs` (multi-file)

Move `shared.rs`, `types.rs`, `test_hooks.rs` out of `workflows/` to a sibling location (e.g. `engine/packages/depot/src/compaction/{shared,types,test_hooks}.rs` or top-level depot module); pick a location consistent with other depot modules
Delete the now-empty `workflows/compaction.rs` umbrella and `workflows/compaction/` subdirectory
Update all `mod` declarations and `use` paths in the depot crate
`cargo test -p depot` passes
`cargo check -p depot` passes
userStories.notes: Identified workflow names from macros: `DbManagerWorkflow` in manager, `DbHotCompacterWorkflow` in hot, `DbColdCompacterWorkflow` in cold, and `DbReclaimerWorkflow` in reclaimer; `companion.rs` is shared loop logic, not a workflow entry. Promoted workflow files to `engine/packages/depot/src/workflows/{db_manager,db_hot_compacter,db_cold_compacter,db_reclaimer}.rs`; moved shared contracts/helpers/test hooks to `engine/packages/depot/src/compaction/{types,shared,companion,test_hooks}.rs`; deleted `workflows/compaction.rs` and the `workflows/compaction/` directory. Preserved the public compatibility facade at `depot::workflows::compaction::*`. Verified `cargo test -p depot` and `cargo check -p depot`.

userStories.title: S9 — Add VFS regression test for crash-recovery between dirty buffer and commit
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a deterministic test that the VFS recovers correctly when the process dies between writing the dirty buffer and ack'ing the commit.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a test in `rivetkit-rust/packages/rivetkit-sqlite/tests/inline/vfs.rs` that simulates a crash with dirty pages still buffered (e.g. drop the `NativeDatabase` mid-flight via the test transport)
Assert post-restart state matches the last successful commit (no torn write visible)
`cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite` passes
userStories.notes: Added `direct_engine_crash_with_dirty_buffer_recovers_last_commit` in `rivetkit-rust/packages/rivetkit-sqlite/tests/inline/vfs.rs`. The test seeds a durable row, injects an `empty_db_page()` into the local VFS dirty buffer, forces the close-time commit to fail before ack via `DirectTransportHooks::fail_next_commit`, reopens on the same direct engine, and asserts the durable row is still visible. Verified `cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite direct_engine_crash_with_dirty_buffer_recovers_last_commit`, `cargo check -p rivetkit-sqlite`, and `cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite` pass with existing Rust 2024 unsafe-operation warnings.

userStories.title: S9 — Add VFS test for concurrent reader during commit-atomic
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a test that a concurrent reader during commit-atomic observes a consistent snapshot — never torn pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a test in `tests/inline/vfs.rs` that spawns a reader thread/task issuing `xRead` while the writer thread is mid-`commit_atomic_write`

Use deterministic gating (Notify or oneshot) to interleave the reader between dirty buffer install and commit ack; assert the reader either sees the pre-commit state or the post-commit state, never partial
`cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite` passes
userStories.notes: Added `concurrent_reader_during_commit_atomic_observes_consistent_snapshot` in `rivetkit-rust/packages/rivetkit-sqlite/tests/inline/vfs.rs`. The test uses `DirectTransportHooks::pause_next_commit` to hold direct commit transport while a writer thread is inside `commit_atomic_write`, then issues `io_read` through the VFS xRead callback on a reader thread and asserts the observed pages are either the full pre-commit snapshot or the full post-commit snapshot, never mixed. Verified `cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite concurrent_reader_during_commit_atomic_observes_consistent_snapshot`, `cargo check -p rivetkit-sqlite`, and `cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite` pass with existing Rust 2024 unsafe-operation warnings.

userStories.title: S9 — Add VFS test for PITR-restore-then-write
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a test that writes after a PITR restore land cleanly on the rolled-back branch.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a test in `tests/inline/vfs.rs` (or a depot integration test if the VFS layer cannot drive restore alone) that performs a PITR restore against a database with prior commits, then issues a fresh write, and asserts the new write is visible on the restored branch
Assert pre-restore commits beyond the restore point are not visible after the new write
`cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite` (or `cargo test -p depot`) passes
userStories.notes: Added Depot integration test `write_after_pitr_restore_lands_on_restored_branch` in `engine/packages/depot/tests/conveyer_restore_point.rs`. The test commits before and after the PITR target, seeds interval coverage, restores by timestamp, writes a new page on the restored branch, and verifies a fresh handle sees the restored page, a zero-filled sparse page instead of the pre-restore page-2 write, and the new post-restore page. The regression exposed that capped ancestor reads could not trust latest parent PIDX after parent commits beyond the cap; `get_pages` now scans capped ancestor DELTA history for requested pages before falling through to SHARD/cold. Verified `cargo test -p depot write_after_pitr_restore_lands_on_restored_branch`, `cargo check -p depot`, and `cargo test -p depot`.

userStories.title: S9 — Add VFS test for fork-and-immediately-reopen
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a test that a fork followed by immediate reopen observes the correct branch state without races.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a test in `tests/inline/vfs.rs` (or depot integration) that forks a database to a new branch and reopens against the new branch in quick succession; assert the reopen sees the pre-fork data and not the parent's later writes
`cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite` (or `cargo test -p depot`) passes
userStories.notes: Added Depot integration test `fork_database_immediate_reopen_isolated_from_parent_later_writes` in `engine/packages/depot/tests/fork_database.rs`. The test commits source pages, forks at that commit, immediately opens a fresh `Db` for the fork and verifies fork-point data/sparse-page state, advances the parent with later writes, then opens another fresh fork `Db` and verifies the fork still sees only the pre-fork snapshot. Verified `cargo test -p depot fork_database_immediate_reopen_isolated_from_parent_later_writes`, `cargo check -p depot`, and `cargo test -p depot`.

userStories.title: S26 — Diagnose RocksDB driver-build flake and remove the retry loop
userStories.description: As a test maintainer, I want the RocksDB driver to construct first-try with a fresh tempdir; the 50×10ms retry loop in `tests/inline/vfs.rs:47-61` papers over a real race.
acceptanceCriteria: Identify the underlying race: each test already gets its own `tempfile::tempdir()` (line 37), so the retry implies shared global state in `RocksDbDatabaseDriver::new`. Document the root cause in story notes
Fix the underlying race in the producer (e.g. remove a global lock, fix lazy init); do NOT add larger waits or different retry shapes — the retry loop must go away
Delete the retry loop at `tests/inline/vfs.rs:47-61`; replace with a single `RocksDbDatabaseDriver::new(...).await?` call
`cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite` passes without the retry
userStories.notes: Added regression test `direct_engine_open_engine_is_concurrency_safe`, which failed before the fix with RocksDB `LOCK` errors (`IO error: lock hold by current process`) when 8 threads concurrently called `DirectEngineHarness::open_engine()` on the same harness. Root cause: not shared tempdirs across tests, but the harness's non-atomic `std::sync::OnceLock` get-then-set lazy init allowed multiple concurrent `RocksDbDatabaseDriver::new(...)` calls for the same tempdir; RocksDB permits only one open handle per path. Replaced the retry loop with `tokio::sync::OnceCell::get_or_init` and a single `RocksDbDatabaseDriver::new(...).await` inside the initializer. Verified `cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite direct_engine_open_engine_is_concurrency_safe -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite`, and `cargo check -p rivetkit-sqlite`.

userStories.title: S25 — Make VFS registration panic-safe via a Drop guard
userStories.description: As a test author, I want a panicking test to leave no leaked SQLite VFS registration in the process-global registry.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a regression test that registers a uniquely named VFS, panics inside `std::panic::catch_unwind`, then asserts `sqlite3_vfs_find(c"<name>")` returns null after the panic

Replace the bare `Drop for SqliteVfs` (`vfs.rs:2212-2219`) cleanup with a guard struct that registers in `new` and unregisters in `Drop`, so the registration is RAII-safe even if the surrounding `NativeDatabase` panics during construction
`cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite` passes
userStories.notes: Added regression test `vfs_registration_is_removed_after_registration_panic`, which registers a unique `panic-leak-vfs-*` VFS, panics inside `std::panic::catch_unwind`, and asserts `sqlite3_vfs_find(...)` returns null after unwinding. Replaced the bare `Drop for SqliteVfs` cleanup with a `SqliteVfsRegistration` Drop guard that owns `sqlite3_vfs_register`/`sqlite3_vfs_unregister`; `SqliteVfs` now keeps the VFS name and boxed context alive until the guard unregisters. Verified `cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite vfs_registration_is_removed_after_registration_panic`, `cargo check -p rivetkit-sqlite`, and `cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite`.

userStories.title: S13 — Wrap NativeDatabase Drop block_on with a bounded timeout
userStories.description: As an actor host shutting down a SQLite database, I should not wedge the Drop thread forever if the envoy WS is gone.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a regression test in `tests/inline/vfs.rs` that constructs a `NativeDatabase` with a `MockProtocol` whose commit future never resolves, drops it from a `std::thread`, joins with a 2-second timeout, and asserts the join completes

Wrap the inner `runtime.block_on(commit_buffered_pages(...))` in `Drop` (`vfs.rs:2236-2266`) with `tokio::time::timeout(<short bound>, ...)`; on timeout, log via `tracing::error!` and return without further blocking
`cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite` passes
userStories.notes: Added regression test `native_database_drop_times_out_pending_commit`, which opens with a normal `MockProtocol`, flips commits to a never-resolving future, drops `NativeDatabase` from a `std::thread`, and asserts completion within 2s. Drop-time buffered commits now go through a short `tokio::time::timeout`; on timeout Drop logs `tracing::error!`, nulls the DB pointer, and returns without calling `sqlite3_close_v2`, which can otherwise re-enter VFS close/flush work. Verified `cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite native_database_drop_times_out_pending_commit`, `cargo check -p rivetkit-sqlite`, and `cargo test -p rivetkit-sqlite`.

userStories.title: S18 — Replace shared mpsc::Receiver with multi-consumer channel for cache-fill workers
userStories.description: As a depot maintainer, I want cache-fill workers to receive jobs in parallel rather than serializing through one `Mutex<Receiver>`.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace `Arc<Mutex<mpsc::Receiver<ShardCacheFillJob>>>` at `engine/packages/depot/src/conveyer/read/cache_fill.rs:86` with `async-channel` (or another multi-consumer pattern); each worker owns its own receiver
Update the worker loop at `cache_fill.rs:199-204` to call `recv().await` on its own receiver, no lock held
Existing cache-fill tests pass: `cargo test -p depot`
`cargo check -p depot` passes
userStories.notes: Replaced `Arc<Mutex<mpsc::Receiver<ShardCacheFillJob>>>` with `async-channel` in `engine/packages/depot/src/conveyer/read/cache_fill.rs`; `ShardCacheFillQueue` now stores cloned `async_channel::Receiver` handles and each worker awaits `receiver.recv().await` directly with no lock. Added the `async-channel` workspace dependency to `depot` and documented the reusable queue pattern in depot AGENTS. Verified `cargo test -p depot` and `cargo check -p depot` pass.

userStories.title: S15 — Make Db::new takeover reconcile non-blocking
userStories.description: As a tokio worker thread, I should not be blocked while `Db::new` runs an FDB scan for the debug-only takeover reconcile.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a test (debug-only via `#[cfg(debug_assertions)]`) that asserts `Db::new` returns within a small bound while a fake FDB scan takes at least 500ms; verify a concurrent CPU task on the same runtime makes progress before `Db::new` returns

Convert `takeover::reconcile_blocking` (`engine/packages/depot/src/takeover.rs:39-57`) to fully async, OR move it to a one-shot background task signaled via `Notify` so `Db::new` does not block the calling worker
Update the call site at `engine/packages/depot/src/conveyer/db.rs:217` accordingly
`cargo test -p depot` passes
`cargo check -p depot` passes
userStories.notes: Added regression test `db_new_does_not_wait_for_takeover_reconcile`, which uses a debug takeover `Notify` gate to hold reconcile pending, verifies a concurrent runtime task makes progress, and asserts `Db::new` returns before the reconcile gate is released. Replaced the debug-only `reconcile_blocking` spawn-and-join wrapper with `reconcile_nonblocking`, which schedules reconcile on the current Tokio runtime or a detached background thread when no runtime is current; `Db::new_inner` now calls the non-blocking scheduler. Verified `cargo test -p depot db_new_does_not_wait_for_takeover_reconcile`, `cargo check -p depot`, `cargo test -p depot --test list_databases parent_tombstone_visibility_is_capped_across_deep_bucket_chain -- --nocapture` after an unrelated transient full-suite failure, and a clean rerun of `cargo test -p depot`.

userStories.title: S22 — Replace timing-magnitude assertion with Promise-gate fixture
userStories.description: As a test author, I want the active-write-count test to assert an exact count rather than a flaky range that depends on host scheduling.
acceptanceCriteria: In `rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit/fixtures/driver-test-suite/sleep-db.ts:872-881`, replace `setTimeout(ACTIVE_DB_WRITE_DELAY_MS)` with a Promise gate the actor `await`s between writes
In `actor-sleep-db.test.ts:1018-1054`, drive the gate from the test: resolve N times, trigger sleep, await shutdown, assert `writeEntries.length === N` (exact)
Replace the `> 0 && < ACTIVE_DB_WRITE_COUNT` range assertion with the deterministic exact-count assertion
`pnpm run check` passes for `rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit`
Vitest filter `actor-sleep-db.test.ts active-db-writes` passes deterministically across 10 runs
userStories.notes: Replaced the active DB write fixture's `ACTIVE_DB_WRITE_DELAY_MS` timer with a WebSocket-driven Promise gate. The actor now waits for `continue-write` permits, sends a `write` ack after each completed insert, and the test advances exactly 3 writes before triggering sleep and asserting `writeEntries.length === 3`. Verified `pnpm vitest run tests/driver/actor-sleep-db.test.ts -t active-db-writes` once and in a 10-run loop. `pnpm run check` is not defined for `rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit`; `pnpm run check-types`, `pnpm biome check fixtures/driver-test-suite/sleep-db.ts tests/driver/actor-sleep-db.test.ts`, `pnpm run check:test-skips`, and `pnpm run check:wait-for-comments` pass. Full `pnpm run lint` is still blocked by pre-existing unrelated Biome diagnostics across package files.

userStories.title: S24 — Add connection.ready Promise to TS client
userStories.description: As a driver-test author, I want a deterministic event to await before issuing actions; today the missing event forces every test to retry-poll.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a `ready: Promise<void>` (or equivalent) to the connection object exposed by `connect()` in the rivetkit TS client (find the right file under `rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit/src/client/`)
`ready` resolves exactly once `isConnected` first flips true; subsequent calls return the same resolved Promise

Add a unit test in `rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit/tests/` asserting `ready` resolves once and exactly when `isConnected` first becomes true; do not use `vi.waitFor` for the assertion (the whole point is to remove the polling justification)
`pnpm run check` passes
`pnpm run check:wait-for-comments` passes
userStories.notes: Added `ActorConnRaw.ready`, backed by a per-connection Promise that resolves from the centralized `#setConnStatus("connected")` transition, so it resolves when `isConnected` first flips true and subsequent reads return the same Promise. Added `ready resolves when connection first opens` in `tests/driver/actor-conn.test.ts`, with no `vi.waitFor`; the test asserts the Promise is pending before the initial connection opens, resolves after the connection becomes connected, and remains the same Promise. Verified `pnpm vitest run tests/driver/actor-conn.test.ts -t "ready resolves when connection first opens"`, `pnpm run check-types`, `pnpm biome check src/client/actor-conn.ts tests/driver/actor-conn.test.ts`, and `pnpm run check:wait-for-comments`. `pnpm run check` is not defined for `rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit`.

userStories.title: S21 — Replace startup vi.waitFor with await connection.ready
userStories.description: As a driver-test author, I want startup-race retry loops replaced with deterministic `await ready` calls now that S24 lands.
acceptanceCriteria: Depends on US-023 (S24)

In `actor-db-stress.test.ts:79-97`, `actor-db.test.ts:144-152`, `actor-db-pragma-migration.test.ts:9-15`, `actor-db-raw.test.ts:74-82`: replace each `vi.waitFor(() => insertBatch/getCount/...)` with `await connection.ready;` followed by a single direct call
Delete the surrounding `// the actor can still be starting` / `// stopping actor instance` justifications now that the polling is gone
`pnpm run check` passes
`pnpm run check:wait-for-comments` passes
Vitest filter for each touched file passes
userStories.notes: Replaced the startup-race `vi.waitFor` wrappers in `actor-db-stress.test.ts`, `actor-db.test.ts`, `actor-db-pragma-migration.test.ts`, and `actor-db-raw.test.ts` with `await connection.ready` followed by a single direct action/read call. Removed the obsolete startup/stopping polling comments and unused ready-timeout constants/imports. Verified `pnpm run check-types`, `pnpm run check:wait-for-comments`, and `pnpm vitest run tests/driver/actor-db-stress.test.ts tests/driver/actor-db.test.ts tests/driver/actor-db-pragma-migration.test.ts tests/driver/actor-db-raw.test.ts` pass. `pnpm run check` is not defined in `rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit`; focused `pnpm biome check` remains blocked by pre-existing formatter/non-null diagnostics in touched files.

userStories.title: S20 — Replace action-polling sleep observer with non-action observer
userStories.description: As a driver-test author, I want to observe actor state after sleep without each observer call resetting the sleep timer.
acceptanceCriteria: Depends on US-023 (S24) for the ready primitive if needed

In `actor-db.test.ts:233-269` (and `:309-322`), replace each `actor.getCount()` poll with a non-action observer (inspector state read OR a lifecycle event hook from the connection); ensure the observer call does not flow through the engine's HTTP request counter and does not reset the sleep timer
Add an assertion that the sleep deadline is not reset by the observer (mock the clock or assert via `last_activity_at`)
`pnpm run check` passes
Vitest filter `actor-db.test.ts persists across sleep` passes
userStories.notes: Replaced the `actor.getCount()` polling loops in `actor-db.test.ts` with a lifecycle observer actor. `dbActorRaw` can now opt into recording `wake`/`sleep` events to `client.lifecycleObserver`, and the sleep test polls that separate observer instead of sending actions to the target actor while waiting. The test asserts `sleepEvent.timestamp - sleepRequestedAt <= SLEEP_OBSERVER_TIMEOUT_MS`, proving observer polling did not reset the target actor's sleep deadline. The hard-crash case now performs one `getCount()` wake/read after `hardCrashActor(...)` instead of retry-looping target actions. Verified `pnpm vitest run tests/driver/actor-db.test.ts -t "persists across sleep"`, `pnpm vitest run tests/driver/actor-db.test.ts -t "hard crash"`, `pnpm run check-types`, `pnpm run check:wait-for-comments`, and focused `pnpm biome check fixtures/driver-test-suite/actor-db-raw.ts tests/driver/actor-db.test.ts`. `pnpm run check` is not defined in `rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit`.

userStories.title: S11 — Pre-arm Notified before re-checking outstanding in wait_idle_for_test
userStories.description: As a depot test author, I want `wait_idle_for_test` to never lose a wakeup when work drains between the load and the await.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a test in `engine/packages/depot/tests/` that drives the lost-wakeup race deterministically: one task in `wait_idle_for_test`; pre-bump `outstanding` to 1; in another task, decrement to 0 and call `notify_waiters()` between the load and the `.notified()` arm via a controlled barrier (e.g. `Notify` + `tokio::task::yield_now`)
Use `tokio::time::timeout(2s, ...)` to surface the bug — test fails today (waiter parks forever)
Fix `engine/packages/depot/src/conveyer/read/cache_fill.rs:133-140`: pin `let n = idle_notify.notified();` (or `Notified::enable`) BEFORE the `outstanding.load()`, then re-check, then `.await`
`cargo test -p depot` passes including the new test
userStories.notes: Added regression test `shard_cache_fill_wait_idle_prearms_before_rechecking_outstanding` in `engine/packages/depot/tests/conveyer_read.rs`. The test pre-bumps shard-cache fill `outstanding` to 1, installs a one-shot hook that fires after `wait_idle_for_test` observes the nonzero count, completes the outstanding work and calls `notify_waiters()` before the waiter awaits, then wraps the waiter in `tokio::time::timeout(2s, ...)`; without pre-arming this parks forever. Fixed `wait_idle_for_test` to create, pin, and enable `idle_notify.notified()` before loading `outstanding`, then re-check and await the pre-armed notification. Verified `cargo test -p depot shard_cache_fill_wait_idle_prearms_before_rechecking_outstanding` and `cargo test -p depot` pass.

userStories.title: S12 — Collapse three-RwLock branch-cache invalidation into single CacheSnapshot
userStories.description: As a concurrent reader on a `Db`, I should never observe a half-updated `(branch_id, ancestors, last_access_bucket)` triple after a DBPTR move.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a test in `engine/packages/depot/tests/conveyer_read.rs` (or sibling) that gates two concurrent `get_pages` around a DBPTR move; assert no observer sees `branch_id == new` while `ancestors == old`. Use deterministic Notify/oneshot gates, no real-clock sleeps
Test fails today (verifier confirmed three sequential `write().await` calls at `read.rs:356-360`)
Replace the three independent `RwLock`s at `db.rs:108,110,114` with a single `RwLock<Option<CacheSnapshot>>` containing all three fields plus the cache
Update `read.rs:353-359` to write the snapshot atomically under one guard, alongside the cache clear
`cargo test -p depot` passes
userStories.notes: Added regression test `branch_cache_snapshot_is_atomic_across_dbptr_move` in `engine/packages/depot/tests/conveyer_read.rs`, which warms the old branch cache, rolls DBPTR to a new branch, starts concurrent `get_pages` calls with a `tokio::sync::Barrier`, and observes that the cache never exposes `branch_id == new` with the old ancestry root. Replaced the three independent `RwLock`s plus standalone PIDX cache with one `RwLock<Option<CacheSnapshot>>` containing branch id, ancestry, access bucket, and PIDX index; `get_pages` and `commit` now publish replacement snapshots under one write lock. Verified `cargo test -p depot branch_cache_snapshot_is_atomic_across_dbptr_move -- --nocapture`, `cargo check -p depot`, and `cargo test -p depot`.

userStories.title: S17 — Cold-tier post-fetch revalidation prevents silent zero-fill
userStories.description: As a depot reader on cold-only data, I should error explicitly if the cold ref has been retired during my read, not silently return zero pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a test in `engine/packages/depot/tests/` (or `cold_tier.rs`) that injects a sleep around `cold_tier.get_object` longer than `delete_after_ms`, runs the reclaimer concurrently to retire the ref, and asserts the read returns `ShardCoverageMissing` (or other typed error), never a zero-filled page
Test fails today: `read.rs:332` zero-fills as `Miss`

After fetching cold bytes in `engine/packages/depot/src/conveyer/read/cold.rs:152-192`, re-validate the cold ref still exists under `Serializable` BEFORE returning bytes; on missing, return `ShardCoverageMissing`
`cargo test -p depot` passes
userStories.notes: Added `cold_ref_retired_during_cold_object_fetch_errors_instead_of_zero_fill` in `engine/packages/depot/tests/conveyer_read.rs`. The test pauses the filesystem cold-tier object read, concurrently retires the compaction cold-shard ref and deletes the cold object, then asserts `get_pages` returns `ShardCoverageMissing` instead of a zero-filled page. Fixed the read path by distinguishing object-missing from page-missing for cold objects; compaction cold-shard object misses now return `ShardCoverageMissing`, and successful cold object fetches re-read the live cold ref under `Serializable` before returning bytes or scheduling shard-cache fill. Verified `cargo test -p depot cold_ref_retired_during_cold_object_fetch_errors_instead_of_zero_fill -- --nocapture`, `cargo check -p depot`, and `cargo test -p depot`.

userStories.title: S30a — Add VFS/depot test for SQLite sparse-page zero-fill semantics
userStories.description: As a depot maintainer, I want a test pinning that pages above any source layer are returned as zero-filled bytes (SQLite spec) and that broken sources error rather than zero-fill.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a depot test asserting: (a) when `db_size_pages > pgno` and no PIDX/DELTA/SHARD/cold source exists, `get_pages` returns a zero page; (b) when a source exists but is broken (corrupted blob), `get_pages` returns an error rather than zero-filling
`cargo test -p depot` passes
userStories.notes: Added `get_pages_zero_fills_sparse_page_without_any_source` and `get_pages_errors_for_corrupted_delta_source` in `engine/packages/depot/tests/conveyer_read.rs`. The sparse test commits a DB with `db_size_pages = 3` and no source for page 2, asserting `get_pages([2])` returns a zero page. The corrupted-source test overwrites the page-1 delta chunk with invalid bytes and asserts `get_pages([1])` errors through `decode source blob for page 1` instead of zero-filling. Verified `cargo test -p depot get_pages_ --test conveyer_read` and `cargo test -p depot`.

userStories.title: S29 — Restore-point creation: re-validate target txid inside tx B
userStories.description: As a user creating a restore point at the edge of expiry, I should never get a Ready handle that resolve_restore_point cannot resolve.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a deterministic test in `engine/packages/depot/tests/conveyer_restore_point.rs`: tx A resolves at edge of expiry; advance time past expiry; trigger reclaim in the gap; tx B writes pin; assert tx B aborts with `RestoreTargetExpired` OR the resulting Ready handle resolves successfully
Test fails today (verifier confirmed two distinct `udb.run` transactions at `pinned.rs:26-40` and `pinned.rs:135`)

Inside `create_restore_point_for_resolved` (`pinned.rs:135`) tx B, add a Serializable read of `branch_commit_key(target.txid)` before writing the pin and abort with `RestoreTargetExpired` if missing — OR fold tx A and tx B into one UDB transaction
`cargo test -p depot` passes
userStories.notes: Added deterministic regression test `create_restore_point_revalidates_target_commit_after_resolve_race` in `engine/packages/depot/tests/conveyer_restore_point.rs`: a debug hook pauses after tx A resolves the timestamp target, the test clears the resolved `COMMITS`, `VTX`, and `PITR_INTERVAL` rows to model the reclaim gap, then tx B resumes and must abort with `RestoreTargetExpired` without writing a Ready restore point. Fixed `create_restore_point_for_resolved` by parsing the restore-point txid and re-reading `branch_commit_key(pin.database_branch_id, restore_point_txid)` under `Serializable` inside tx B before writing the restore-point record, DB pin, or branch pin. Verified `cargo test -p depot create_restore_point_revalidates_target_commit_after_resolve_race -- --nocapture`, `cargo check -p depot`, and `cargo test -p depot`.

userStories.title: S30b — restore_database: fold rollback + undo restore-point write into one UDB tx
userStories.description: As a user restoring a database, I should never end up in a state where DBPTR has rolled but no undo restore point is durable.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a fault-injection test in `engine/packages/depot/tests/restore_points.rs` (or sibling) that simulates a process crash between rollback and undo-rp write; on restart, assert all-or-nothing: either DBPTR is unchanged AND no undo rp, OR DBPTR rolled AND undo rp is durable
Test fails today (verifier confirmed four separate `udb.run` calls at `restore.rs:14-29`)
Fold steps 3 and 4 (`branch::rollback_database_to_target` + `create_restore_point_for_resolved`) into a single UDB transaction
`cargo test -p depot` passes
userStories.notes: Added regression test `restore_database_rollback_and_undo_pin_are_atomic` in `engine/packages/depot/tests/conveyer_restore_point.rs`. The test injects a failure after the rollback transaction work but before undo restore-point pinning, then asserts DBPTR remains on the old branch and no undo restore point is durable; with the old two-transaction shape this seam would leave rolled-without-undo state. Refactored rollback and restore-point pin creation into transaction-level helpers (`rollback_database_to_target_tx` and `create_restore_point_for_resolved_tx`) and made `restore_database` run both inside one UDB transaction. Also made restore-point debug hooks one-shot and gave hooked tests unique database ids so parallel tests cannot steal or park on hooks. Verified `cargo test -p depot restore_database_rollback_and_undo_pin_are_atomic -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p depot --test conveyer_restore_point -- --nocapture`, `cargo check -p depot`, and `cargo test -p depot`.

userStories.title: S32 — Add shard-cache eviction regression test for pin at future txid
userStories.description: As a depot maintainer, I want a test that pins satisfied via cold-coverage stay satisfied after eviction releases their cache row.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a depot integration test: pin at `t2 > newest_shard.as_of_txid (t1)`; trigger eviction of the SHARD at `t1`; read at `t2`; assert the read succeeds via the cold path (uses `CMP/cold_shard`)
Coverage gap fix only: confirm the test would fail if the cold-coverage gate at `shared.rs:851-854` regressed
`cargo test -p depot` passes
userStories.notes: Added Depot workflow integration test `reclaimer_eviction_preserves_future_pin_reads_via_cold_ref` in `engine/packages/depot/tests/workflow_compaction_skeletons.rs`. The test creates a real database, pins txid 2 with a restore point, seeds a matching cold-backed SHARD at txid 1, forces reclaim to evict the FDB SHARD row, clears the hot PIDX row for page 1 so the final read must use `CMP/cold_shard`, and asserts the nonzero page remains readable. This pins the cold-coverage safety invariant for future-pin eviction; if matching cold coverage is skipped or cold-backed candidates stop evicting, the SHARD eviction/read assertions fail. Verified `cargo test -p depot reclaimer_eviction_preserves_future_pin_reads_via_cold_ref -- --nocapture`, `cargo check -p depot`, and `cargo test -p depot`.

userStories.title: S23a — Add Gasoline WorkflowCreated bump subject (core change)
userStories.description: As a depot test author, I want a Gasoline subscription primitive for new workflow rows so tests can wait deterministically instead of polling.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a `WorkflowCreated { tag: String }` (or by-name equivalent) variant to `BumpSubSubject` in `gasoline/src/db/mod.rs:362-366`
Wire the new subject into the existing Gasoline bump-publish path so workflow creation publishes to it
Add a unit test in `engine/packages/gasoline/tests/` asserting a subscriber to `WorkflowCreated { tag }` receives a notification when a workflow with that tag is created
`cargo test -p gasoline` passes
`cargo check -p gasoline` passes
userStories.notes: Added `BumpSubSubject::WorkflowCreated { tag }`, encoded tags safely into pubsub subjects with hex, and published the subject after top-level workflow and sub-workflow creation commits. Unique dispatches that find an existing workflow do not emit a created bump. Added `engine/packages/gasoline/tests/db_bump.rs::workflow_created_bump_fires_for_workflow_tag`, which subscribes before dispatching a tagged workflow and asserts the bump arrives. Updated `.agent/notes/sqlite-review-issues.md` to mark the Gasoline core blocker done while leaving the Depot polling rewrite open. `cargo test -p gasoline` initially exposed an existing broken `join_signal!` doctest; fixed the doc snippet so the required full package test passes. Verified `cargo test -p gasoline workflow_created_bump_fires_for_workflow_tag`, `cargo test -p gasoline`, and `cargo check -p gasoline`.

userStories.title: S23b — Use WorkflowCreated subscription in workflow_compaction_skeletons.rs wait_until
userStories.description: As a depot test author, I want `wait_until` for workflow rows to subscribe to the Gasoline notification rather than polling on a 25ms real-clock loop.
acceptanceCriteria: Depends on US-033 (S23a)
Add a test helper `wait_for_workflow_row(tag)` (or extend the existing `wait_until` helper) that subscribes to `BumpSubSubject::WorkflowCreated { tag }` and resolves on the first matching publish
Replace the real-clock polling at `engine/packages/depot/tests/workflow_compaction_skeletons.rs:184-206` with the new helper across all callers in that file
`cargo test -p depot` passes; the suite is faster (no 25ms polling tax)
userStories.notes: Replaced the workflow-row wait path in `engine/packages/depot/tests/workflow_compaction_skeletons.rs`: `wait_for_workflow` now subscribes to `BumpSubSubject::WorkflowCreated { tag }` before checking `find_workflow`, then waits on bumps instead of the 25ms polling loop. The generic `wait_until` remains for signal debug rows and UDB observation rows that do not expose notifications, and depot AGENTS now records the updated skeleton-test convention. Verified `cargo test -p depot --test workflow_compaction_skeletons manager_spawns_companions_and_records_deltas_available -- --nocapture`, `cargo test -p depot`, and `cargo check -p depot`.

userStories.title: Add Depot inspect API with big metadata JSON and paginated row scans
userStories.description: As a Rivet operator, I want an internal api-peer Depot inspect surface that returns decoded bucket/database/branch metadata in bounded JSON blobs, with pagination for unbounded row families.
acceptanceCriteria: Use `.agent/specs/depot-inspect-api.md` as the source of truth for route shape and response contents
Add api-peer Depot inspect routes under `/depot/inspect/...`; do not expose them through api-public or generated public SDKs
The bucket, database, and branch metadata routes return one bounded decoded JSON response with summaries and samples rather than many separate basic-metadata endpoints
All unbounded scans are paginated: catalog, branch rows via `/depot/inspect/branches/{branch_id}/rows/{family}`, and raw scans
The branch rows endpoint supports the families listed in the spec and enforces a default limit of 100 and hard cap of 1000
Raw key and raw scan endpoints use unpadded base64url keys and include best-effort Depot key/value decode output
Add focused tests for route registration, pagination limit enforcement, cursor behavior, and at least one decoded row family
`cargo check -p rivet-api-peer -p depot` passes
`cargo test -p depot` passes
userStories.notes: Added the internal api-peer `/depot/inspect/...` surface backed by `depot::inspect`, including summary, paginated catalog, big bucket/database/branch blobs, page trace, paginated branch row-family scans, and raw key/scan/decode endpoints. Branch rows support the spec families with default limit 100 and hard cap 1000; raw keys/cursors use unpadded base64url and include best-effort key/value decode. Added focused Depot tests for pagination limit enforcement, cursor behavior, raw scan cursor use, and decoded `pidx` rows, plus an api-peer route registration test. Verified `cargo check -p depot -p rivet-api-peer`, `cargo test -p depot --test inspect`, `cargo test -p rivet-api-peer --test depot_inspect`, and `cargo test -p depot`.
